item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty accurate portrayal of the way that extremely-on-reddit-types in 2012 would go on and on about how misremembering the less-common spelling of an author's name, or the name of a forgettable mid-90s children's movie, was somehow proof of information leaking through from alternate dimensions. From the comments, it seems like there used to be a jump scare in this? Probably for the best that it's gone; I feel like this works best as scary for the person under its effect, but funny for the reader trying to imagine a threatening, anomalous Tom Hanks.

item.title: FANART????
item.content:encoded:
+ Reveal Image
- Collapse Image

This fanart is fantastic, courtesy of the 2020 Gift Exchange!! Artist is Luxaiko, definitely go check out their works!!
I didn't expect this to last a day, let alone survive up to a whopping +100! I'm glad people enjoyed this hilariously ridiculous concept!

item.title: Forrest did meet Presidents though
item.content:encoded:
Gump sat alone on a bench in a park,
Beginning his life story with a casual remark,
Now I'm losing my mind and I can't stop it,
Ever since I heard the line about a box of chocolates.

He's GUMP! He's GUMP! He's GUMP!
He's in my head...

I pondered a bit after finishing the article, but decided that this gets a +1. Good work!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: im suprised !! Somehow this page got better and better the more i read. Perfect mix of goofy and terrifying. Easy +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly really terrifying, keep up the good work! +1

item.title: Run forest.... run
item.content:encoded: Wow this is my favorite 5000+ article. Its so well written, and as a fan of the movie it gave me chills.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well if you told me before I saw this I'd be reading a Forrest Gump SCP, not filed under Jokes, that made sense, I'd… I'd laugh.

And like, it was going fine, reading the article right until discourse over "life is a" and "life was a" "box of chocolates".

I could've sworn it was "is", not "was", and this is pulling the same unnerving stuff SCP-096 did to me when I first read its article.

Kudos for that. +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've only read the current version of this one, and I like it. The inclusion of the deepfakes was a good laugh at the beginning, but the slow ramp into the terror of being stalked by something always gets me.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this scp was a trip and a half. its funny but i have literally had this conversation that the line is always misquoted before. made it extra creepy for me, but still funny. i love thenote at the end. i can't stop imagining forest gump saying it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Run, Forrest, run!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The repeated collapsibles to "check vitals" were gimmicky back in SCP-2316 and are even more so now. It's instantly obvious how they're going to progress and this didn't stray from that pattern at all. eta: these have now been removed.

Beyond that, the actual horror really doesn't land, since the (pretty decent) exploration into the mandela effect is dropped in favour of a generic jumpscare, and one which really isn't even that scary (Tom Hanks is probably one of the least scary people alive).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel like the end was fairly predictable didn't deliver quite as much punch on the premise as it could have, but the premise itself is so goofy that it makes it an impressive little horror story regardless. This was a pretty creative use of the Mandela effect, which I would have worried was overused before this story. So +1

I will say that while I guess I kinda got the jumpscare picture's purpose, I honestly think the article might be stronger without it, or with something less obviously, superficially scary. The way it's presented, it almost looks like the picture is the punchline to the article, and yet, it's not the reason I enjoyed the article.
This is so unbelievably goofy yet scary at the same time and a great twist on the whole "no, I remember it this way" argument. Very well done, easy upvote!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't know if this is supposed to be funny; but the posts about the mandela effect really hit home for me as everything around this is just… so funny around me, from the fact parawatch is making a bunch of conspiracy theories about a possibly just misheard line; to this being the SCP universe and there actually being something there and just everything!

That, coupled with the actually pretty sinister and creepy ending gets a +1 from me, good job!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I want to congratulate you on writing something that is both hilarious and also chillingly creepy. Well done!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Forrest Gump? More like:

Forrest Gunna Get'cha!

item.title: whats a forrest gump?
item.content:encoded: Seeing the before and after of this article is a difference that cannot be understated. The vitals check does remind me of SCP-2316, but it doesn't detract from the horror of it. Good job, Jak! (+1)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This made me laugh much more than I wanted to. Thank you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Everyone here likes the picture but I personally found it very silly. All the darkness in the world doesn't change the fact that it's Tom Hanks peering at you menacingly through a window.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ending was a little cliche, but that picture. Holy hell, that gave me chills. Great lead up to a terrifying image.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ok, so I understood the obsession part. But, I don't really understand the last bit.about him. Anyway, nice article Jak +1
I don't know what I expected going into this, but it definitely wasn't that. Even gave me a slight chill down my spine at the end, excellent job!

item.title: The Gumpiracy
item.content:encoded:
The fact that this has stayed on after 24 hours is frankly astonishing.

I want to thank the dozens of critters for reading this article. This includes Pedagon, Grigori Karpin, KumoTheSpider, etoisle, JackalRelated, Veralta, Flopmind, and the several on #thecritters. (Just let me know if I missed you and I'll add you on!)

Thanks for your time!
